mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as indicated by increasing non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy economy that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:US labor market strength in late 2021
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth rate is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, exceeds a critical threshold, it signals increased pressure on the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL > 275 but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and robust industrial production indicates broad-based economic strength, suggesting healthy corporate revenues and profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND INDPRO growth > 0.3% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending and industrial activity in late 2021
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and INDPRO growth conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime, any further significant decline in the unemployment rate signals an overheating labor market, potentially leading to wage inflation and tighter monetary policy, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 4.0% and 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances within a full employment regime.

mechanism_narrative: A significant slowdown in housing starts indicates weakening demand and potential economic contraction, leading to concerns about future corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market sensitivity to economic cycles
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below 1500k units but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low federal funds rates signal an accommodative monetary environment, which generally supports asset valuations by lowering the cost of capital and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low interest rate environment post-GFC and during COVID recovery
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6 consecutive months, but the average 20-day S&P 500 return during this period is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is strong but the federal funds rate remains low, it indicates a supportive economic backdrop with ample liquidity, boosting investor confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-pandemic economic recovery with low rates
failure_condition: If GDPC1 YoY growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader supply-side weakness, which negatively impacts corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 0.5% MoM for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% MoM for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 7-year rolling window.
